+- **Conditional references preserved**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` in Api.csproj uses TFM-conditional `<PackageVersion>` entries (net6.0 → 6.0.36, net8.0 → 8.0.11)
+
+## Section 2: Version Conflict Resolutions
+
+Three version conflicts were resolved by aligning to the highest version (per user request):
+
+### System.Text.Json
+
+| Version | Projects |
+|---------|----------|
+| 10.0.1 | Api, Web, Tests |
+| 9.0.0 | Core |
+| 8.0.5 | Legacy |
+
+**Resolution**: Aligned to **10.0.1** (highest). Core and Legacy now resolve a higher version than before.
+
+### Azure.Storage.Blobs
+
+| Version | Projects |
+|---------|----------|
+| 12.24.0 | Api (via `$(BlobsVersion)`) |
+| 12.19.0 | Legacy |
+
+**Resolution**: Aligned to **12.24.0** (highest). Legacy now resolves 12.24.0 instead of 12.19.0 (minor version bump).
+
+### Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson
+
+| Version | Context |
+|---------|---------|
+| 6.0.36 | Api — net6.0 TFM only |
+| 8.0.11 | Api — net8.0 TFM only |
+
+**Resolution**: Not a true conflict — these are TFM-conditional by design. Handled with conditional `<PackageVersion>` entries in `Directory.Packages.props`, preserving the original per-framework versions.
+
+## Section 3: Package Comparison — Baseline vs. Result
+
+### Changes
+
+| Project | Package | Before | After | Status |
+|---------|---------|--------|-------|--------|
+| Core.csproj | System.Text.Json | 9.0.0 | 10.0.1 | Aligned to highest version |
+| Legacy.csproj | System.Text.Json | 8.0.5 | 10.0.1 | Aligned to highest version |
+| Legacy.csproj | Azure.Storage.Blobs | 12.19.0 | 12.24.0 | Aligned to highest version |
+
+### Unchanged
+
+| Project | Package | Version |
+|---------|---------|---------|
+| Api.csproj (net6.0) | Azure.Storage.Blobs | 12.24.0 |
+| Api.csproj (net6.0) | Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson | 6.0.36 |
+| Api.csproj (net6.0) | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Api.csproj (net6.0) | System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 |
+| Api.csproj (net8.0) | Azure.Storage.Blobs | 12.24.0 |
+| Api.csproj (net8.0) | Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson | 8.0.11 |
+| Api.csproj (net8.0) | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Api.csproj (net8.0) | System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 |
+| Core.csproj | Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | 8.0.1 |
+| Core.csproj | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Tests.csproj | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Tests.csproj | System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 |
+| Tests.csproj | xunit | 2.9.3 |
+| Legacy.csproj | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Web.csproj | Microsoft.Extensions.Logging | 9.0.0 |
+| Web.csproj | OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | 1.15.0 |
+| Web.csproj | System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 |
+
+## Section 4: Risk Assessment
+
+**[Moderate risk]** — Three packages changed resolved versions due to alignment to the highest version:
+
+- **System.Text.Json**: Core (9.0.0 → 10.0.1) and Legacy (8.0.5 → 10.0.1) — this is a **major version bump** for both projects. Review [System.Text.Json 10.x release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/core/whats-new/dotnet-10/runtime#json) for breaking changes.
+- **Azure.Storage.Blobs**: Legacy (12.19.0 → 12.24.0) — minor version bump within the same major. Lower risk but should still be validated.
+
+Recommend running `dotnet test` across all projects to verify no regressions, especially in Core and Legacy.
+
+## Section 5: Follow-Up Items
+
+1. **Run `dotnet test`** to validate runtime behavior, especially for Core and Legacy which received version bumps for System.Text.Json and Azure.Storage.Blobs.
+2. **Review System.Text.Json 10.x breaking changes** — Core was on 9.0.0 and Legacy was on 8.0.5; both now resolve 10.0.1. Check for API changes that may affect these projects.
+3. **net6.0 end-of-support**: Api.csproj multi-targets net6.0 and Legacy.csproj targets net6.0. Both produce NETSDK1138 warnings. Consider migrating to a supported TFM.
+4. **TFM-support warnings**: Several transitive packages (Microsoft.Extensions.*, System.Text.Json, System.IO.Pipelines) warn about net6.0 incompatibility. These existed before the conversion but are worth addressing.
+
+## Section 6: Artifacts and How to Use Them
+
+- **`baseline.binlog`** — MSBuild binary log captured before CPM conversion
+- **`after-cpm.binlog`** — MSBuild binary log captured after CPM conversion
+- **`baseline-packages.json`** — Resolved package versions per project before conversion
+- **`after-cpm-packages.json`** — Resolved package versions per project after conversion
+- **`convert-to-cpm.md`** — This report file
+
+Binary logs can be opened with the [MSBuild Structured Log Viewer](https://msbuildlog.com/) for detailed build comparison and troubleshooting. Run `dotnet test` to validate runtime behavior beyond build success.
+

**5 projects converted**, **8 packages centralized** into `Directory.Packages.props`.

**What changed:**
- **MSBuild properties inlined & removed**: `$(BlobsVersion)` and `$(HostingVersion)` from `Directory.Build.props`
- **Common.props**: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging` version removed (now central)
- **TFM-conditional versions preserved**: `Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc.NewtonsoftJson` uses conditional `<PackageVersion>` entries (net6.0 → 6.0.36, net8.0 → 8.0.11)

**Version conflicts resolved (highest wins):**
| Package | Before | After | Affected |
|---------|--------|-------|----------|
| System.Text.Json | 8.0.5 / 9.0.0 | 10.0.1 | Core, Legacy |
| Azure.Storage.Blobs | 12.19.0 | 12.24.0 | Legacy |

**Risk**: Moderate — major version bump for System.Text.Json in Core/Legacy. Run `dotnet test` to validate. Full details in `convert-to-cpm.md`.
